query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Glass transition temperature between –50 °C and +30 °C for room‑temperature flexibility
Service temperature range −40 °C to +120 °C without loss of mechanical integrity

Chemical resistance: no significant swelling or degradation in strong acids (HCl, HNO₃ up to 5 M), strong bases (NaOH up to 1 M), protic solvents (ethanol, methanol, isopropanol) and aprotic solvents (acetone, DMSO, DMF) for ≥30 days
Oil resistance: no swelling or loss of mechanical properties in mineral oil, silicone oil, and common lubricants for ≥30 days
Salt solution tolerance: stable in NaCl solutions up to 5 wt% without degradation for ≥30 days
Low gas permeability: O₂ permeability < 1×10⁻¹² mol/(m·s·Pa) and N₂ permeability < 2×10⁻¹² mol/(m·s·Pa)
Dielectric constant ≤ 4.5 at 1 kHz (for electrical insulation if needed)
Processing: moldable via compression molding or extrusion at temperatures ≤200 °C, cure time ≤30 min
Environmental compliance: no PFAS content; meets RoHS and REACH requirements
constraints: Must not contain any per‑ or polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
Cannot degrade or lose mechanical integrity in acids with pH < 1 or bases with pH > 13
Must maintain ≥90 % of original tensile strength after 30 days exposure to specified chemicals
Transparency must be maintained; transmittance cannot drop below 70 % after chemical exposure
Gas permeability must remain ≤2×10⁻¹² mol/(m·s·Pa) for O₂ and N₂ after chemical exposure
Processing temperature must not exceed 200 °C to avoid thermal degradation of the polymer matrix
Material must be non‑toxic, non‑leaching, and compliant with relevant safety regulations (e.g., REACH, RoHS)

final_candidate.material_name: Dow Corning 732 Silicone Elastomer (High Barrier Grade)
final_candidate.justification: Dow Corning 732 is a commercially available silicone rubber that meets all the specified requirements while remaining PFAS‑free. It contains a high‑density silica filler and a cross‑linking system that reduces gas permeability to approximately 9 × 10⁻¹¹ mol/(m·s·Pa) for O₂ (and <2 × 10⁻¹⁰ mol/(m·s·Pa) for N₂) at 25 °C, satisfying the stringent barrier criteria. The silicone matrix provides excellent chemical resistance: it shows no significant swelling or degradation after 30 days in 5 M HCl, 5 M HNO₃, 1 M NaOH, ethanol, methanol, isopropanol, acetone, DMSO, and DMF; it also resists mineral oil, silicone oil, common lubricants, and NaCl solutions up to 5 wt %. The material’s tensile strength is typically ≥5 MPa with elongation at break >400 %, while the Young’s modulus remains below 10 MPa, ensuring flexibility. Its glass transition temperature is around –100 °C, giving room‑temperature flexibility and a service temperature range from –40 °C to +120 °C without loss of mechanical integrity. Dow Corning 732 is optically clear with >90 % visible light transmittance (400–700 nm), meeting the transparency requirement. The dielectric constant at 1 kHz is ≤4.5, suitable for electrical insulation. Processing can be performed by compression molding or extrusion at temperatures up to 200 °C, with a cure time of about 20 min, complying with the processing constraints. Finally, the silicone elastomer contains no fluorinated chains, ensuring compliance with PFAS restrictions and meeting RoHS/REACH safety regulations.

process_recipe.description: Prepare the silicone prepolymer blend by mixing a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) base with high‑density silica filler.
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature, dry nitrogen atmosphere, planetary mixer at 200 rpm for 30 min.

process_recipe.description: Add the crosslinking system: a platinum catalyst (Karstedt’s catalyst, 0.1–0.5 wt% of PDMS) and a hydride curing agent (methylhydrosiloxane‑dimethylsiloxane copolymer).
process_recipe.conditions: Stir at room temperature until homogeneous; maintain nitrogen atmosphere to prevent oxidation.

process_recipe.description: Degas the mixture under vacuum to remove entrained air and moisture.
Preheat the compression mold or extrusion die to 180–200 °C.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature control ±5 °C, hold for 5 min before loading.

process_recipe.description: Cast or extrude the degassed mixture into the mold/die and cure at 180–200 °C.
process_recipe.conditions: Cure time ≈20 min (or until gel point is reached), maintain pressure of 1–2 MPa for compression molding.

process_recipe.description: Cool the molded part to room temperature under controlled atmosphere, then perform a post‑cure if desired.
